title: Verizon ups wireless subscriber guidance on surprise Q1 adds
content: Verizon posted positive wireless postpaid phone net adds for the first time since 2013 as CEO Dan Schulman's plan takes shape, but January's network outage dented service revenue growth.

title: Analysts weigh what's next for cable after 'Bloody Friday'
content: Charter's and Comcast's shares took a beating on what one analyst called 'Bloody Friday.' While the size of the sell-off might not have been warranted, it is fueling speculation about M&A and further cable sector consolidation.

title: Verizon surpasses 6M FWA subs as priority shifts to fiber
content: Amid Verizon's expanding convergence strategy, there will be a bigger mix-shift to fiber broadband over FWA, said CEO Dan Schulman. Verizon, he added, will look to accelerate the expansion of its fiber footprint via partnerships and potential M&A.

title: The only convergence lever that works is the one you buried
content: Across nearly 1.4 million survey responses, one variable predicts wireless market share better than brand, price or network quality: whether the carrier owns the fiber under the street.

title: Telin and DITO join forces to bolster cross-border connectivity
content: The two companies agreed to strengthen regional connectivity between the Philippines and Indonesia, underscoring their shared commitment to expanding their networks and improving their services.

title: Household gigabit subscribers to reach 60% by 2030 – Omdia
content: Residential households subscribing to gigabit broadband will increase from about one-third in 2025 to 60% in 2030, according to Omdia analysts, putting pressure on ISPs to deliver higher speeds without compromising revenue.
